# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3'
services:

  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:${esVersion} 
    ports:
      - 9200:9200 # ELK service is accessible over port 9200 (in network host mode the exposed port is mapped to port 443)
      - 9300:9300
    environment: 
      - xpack.security.enabled=false 
    volumes:
        - /srv/elasticsearch:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data # volume is defined here

  kibana: 
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:${esVersion}
    ports:
      - 5601:5601 # Kibana service exposed on port 5601 (in network host mode the exposed port is mapped to port 443)

  logstash: 
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:${esVersion}
    ports: 
      - 9280:9280 # ELK service accessible over port 9280 (in network host mode the exposed port is mapped to port 5671)
      - 9380:9380 # ELK service accessible over port 9380 (in network host mode the exposed port is mapped to port 443)
    links: 
      - elasticsearch 

  app: 
    image: ${registry-url}/${app-repo}:${app-version}
    ports:
      - 127.0.0.56:${app-port}:980 # app service accessible over internal IP and exposed on port ${app-port}, ELK stack also accessible in this port (in network host mode the exposed port is mapped to port 443)
    environment: 
      - env_app_id=${app-env}
      - logstash.host=logstash-service:9280 # ELK service available through host and container name
      - elastalert.url=http://apm-server-service:5671/elastalert # apm server service accessible over host
    links: 
      - logstash 
      - elasticsearch 
      - app-db
